description_long: This is a bronze-colored Corinthian-style helmet with a prominent black crest running from front to back along the top. The helmet features a rounded dome with a pronounced brow ridge and angular cheek guards that extend downward, partially covering the face. The surface is slightly weathered, showing scuffs and patina, giving it an aged, battle-worn appearance. The crest is made of stiff, bristled material, likely horsehair or synthetic fibers, and is mounted in a central groove. The eye openings are narrow and angular, providing limited but focused visibility. There are no visible ventilation holes, logos, or modern retention systems, and the interior is likely hollow to accommodate a human head.
description: A bronze Corinthian-style helmet with a black crest, angular cheek guards, and a weathered, metallic finish.
description_view_0: Front view shows the black crest running centrally and the rounded dome with cheek guards barely visible.
description_view_1: Left side view highlights the full length of the black crest, the dome shape, and the angular cheek guard with a rectangular eye opening.
description_view_2: Direct front view reveals the narrow eye slits, pronounced brow, and symmetrical cheek guards.
description_view_3: Right side view mirrors the left, showing the crest, dome, and cheek guard with a rectangular eye opening.
